acceptance_criteria_for_revision: axi_assertion_gap_report.md contains a remediation recommendation table with chosen option(s), per-option costs, combined cost, and percent-of-budget calculation.
A combos table enumerates all considered combos with feasibility flags and the chosen combo is justified as 'smallest sufficient' by explicit acceptance criteria or cost-effectiveness rule.
counterexample_table.csv (or companion CSV) includes chosen_action flags and cost attribution where relevant.
Artifacts are complete (no truncation) and the grader/verifier can locate the remediation selection directly in the report.
axi_assertion_gap_report.md contains a remediation recommendation table with chosen option(s), per-option costs, combined cost, and percent-of-budget calculation.
A combos table enumerates all considered combos with feasibility flags and the chosen combo is justified as 'smallest sufficient' by explicit acceptance criteria or cost-effectiveness rule.
counterexample_table.csv (or companion CSV) includes chosen_action flags and cost attribution where relevant.
Artifacts are complete (no truncation) and the grader/verifier can locate the remediation selection directly in the report.

planned_changes: Untruncate and expand axi_assertion_gap_report.md to include a dedicated 'Remediation Recommendation' section containing: chosen option(s) (IDs), per-option cost, combined cost, percent of remediation_budget_usd used, and an explicit statement of acceptance criteria or demonstration that the remediation is 'sufficient'.

Add a 'Combos Considered' table to the report enumerating all option combinations, their combined cost, combined reduction_pct (or combined effect metric), and feasibility flag (cost <= remediation_budget_usd).

Add a short justification for selecting the particular combo marked as 'smallest sufficient' - either by showing it meets a documented acceptance threshold or by comparing marginal effectiveness per cost and documenting the decision rule (e.g., minimize cost subject to meeting acceptance delta).

Append or update counterexample_table.csv (or add companion CSV) to flag records affected by the recommended remediation and include columns for chosen_action, action_cost_share, and post-remediation proxy if applicable.
Include the console/trace combos output (or a compact derivative table) in the report appendix so graders can trace the computational steps that led to the recommendation.

Run a quick sensitivity check on the remediation decision by varying the provisional acceptance threshold (e.g., +/- 5-10%) and report whether the recommended remediation remains preferred; document results concisely.
Regenerate artifacts and verify the full report file is present (not truncated) before submission.
raw_llm_output.revision_reason: Add an explicit, auditable remediation recommendation and cost justification (the grader deducted credit because the chosen remediation and combined-cost math were not clearly presented in the artifacts; report preview was truncated).

metadata_json.revision_goal: Artifacts are present, calculations (burst_bytes, priority_proxy) and +5% sensitivity analysis are reproducible and documented. Partial credit deducted because the submission did not include an explicit, auditable remediation recommendation (selected option(s), combined cost, percent-of-budget check, and justification of 'smallest sufficient' or acceptance criteria). The report preview was truncated which reduced grader confidence in remediation selection claims.
